title: angianthus humifusus var. grandiflorus
intro: Angianthus humifusus var. grandiflorus, commonly known as the large-flowered white daisy bush, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to Australia. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate a range of health issues. The health benefits of Angianthus humifusus var. grandiflorus include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and relieving respiratory problems, among others. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
